primarySourceLabel: UW Latin American and Caribbean Studies major requirements
sourceLabel: UW Latin American and Caribbean Studies major requirements
requirementCueLines: The Latin American and Caribbean Studies major combines language study in Spanish and Portuguese with work in history, the humanities, and the social sciences. It provides a comprehensive, interdisciplinary understanding of this major world region, emphasizing themes such as economic development, popular movements, critical analysis, and hemispheric relations.
The Latin American and Caribbean Studies Major consists of 52 credit hours from the below requirements.
JSIS 201 The Making of the 21st Century SSc 5

Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces.
HSTCMP 247 Global Health Histories: Colonial Medicine, Public Health, And International Health In The Global South DIV, SSc 5
HSTLAC 185 Race, Gender, And Class In Latin America And The Caribbean DIV, SSc 5
HSTLAC 280 Drug Wars In Latin America DIV, SSc 5
HSTLAC 282 History Of Mexico: Culture, Identity, And The Politics Of Rule From The Aztecs To The Present DIV, SSc 5
HSTLAC 481 History Of Peru And The Andean Region SSc 5
HSTLAC 482 The History Of Brazil: Colonial Period To The Present SSc 5
JSIS 396 Study Abroad: Latin American Studies SSc 1-5
For participants in study abroad program. Specific course content varies. Courses do not automatically apply to major/minor requirements.
JSIS A 323 Inter-American Relations SSc, DIV 5
JSIS A 324 Human Rights in Latin America SSc, DIV 5
JSIS A 325 Modern Mexico: Culture, Politics And Society SSc 5
JSIS A 342 Government and Politics of Latin America SSc 5
JSIS A 354 Narcoculture: Propaganda and Publicity in the War on Drugs SSc 5
JSIS A 365 Mapping Luso-Brazilian Cultures SSc, A&H 5
* JSIS 396 Latin America Study Abroad (when contemporary LA content)
Every quarter, LACS posts a current list of qualifying elective courses offered across campus.
AES 442 Undocumented Immigrant Communities DIV, SSc 5
CHSTU 224 Life And Labor In The US-Mexico Borderlands DIV, SSc 5
DANCE 490 History and Development of Afro-Peruvian Dance A&H 3
GEOG 403 Transnational Latinx Migrations DIV, SSc 5
Internship (if Latin America focus)(max 5 credits applied to maj/minor)
JSIS A 492 Latin American Studies Seminar SSc 5

supportLists.listTitle: Every quarter, LACS posts a current list of qualifying elective courses offered across campus.

parsedRequirementCourses.courseCode: HSTCMP 247 Global Health Histories: Colonial Medicine, Public Health, And International Health In The Global South DIV, SSc 5
parsedRequirementCourses.title: Traces the roots of the modern global health movement by examining the history of overseas interventions in medicine and public health from the fifteenth century to the present. Focuses primarily on Latin America while including case studies on Africa, Asia, Australia, and the Pacific.
parsedRequirementCourses.sourceHeading: Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces
parsedRequirementCourses.sourceCategory: Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces

parsedRequirementCourses.courseCode: HSTLAC 185 Race, Gender, And Class In Latin America And The Caribbean DIV, SSc 5
parsedRequirementCourses.title: Explores the power struggles generated by imperial expansion, colonialism, and processes of nation-formation (1400 to the present) in the forging of Latin America and the Caribbean. Examines how gender, race, sex, and class became crucial means to sustain, navigate, and challenge domination as well as to imagine alternative futures.
parsedRequirementCourses.sourceHeading: Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces
parsedRequirementCourses.sourceCategory: Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces

parsedRequirementCourses.courseCode: HSTLAC 280 Drug Wars In Latin America DIV, SSc 5
parsedRequirementCourses.title: Analyses "War on Drugs" in Latin America as political, economic, and socio-cultural construct. Investigates local, regional and global dynamics rendering some mood-altering substances as legal while subjecting others to prohibitionist policies at different historical times. Explores racial, class and gender logics shaping these processes. Examines how informal economies are well entrenched within longstanding, transnational institutions.
parsedRequirementCourses.sourceHeading: Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces
parsedRequirementCourses.sourceCategory: Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces

parsedRequirementCourses.courseCode: HSTLAC 282 History Of Mexico: Culture, Identity, And The Politics Of Rule From The Aztecs To The Present DIV, SSc 5
parsedRequirementCourses.title: Overview of Mexican history from late Aztec times until the twenty-first century. Emphasizes how women, campesinos, indigenous populations, free and enslaved Afro-Mexicans, and the urban poor experienced the past, challenged colonial and post-colonial rule, and shaped modern Mexican society and culture.
parsedRequirementCourses.sourceHeading: Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces
parsedRequirementCourses.sourceCategory: Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces

parsedRequirementCourses.courseCode: HSTLAC 481 History Of Peru And The Andean Region SSc 5
parsedRequirementCourses.title: Traces the history of Peru specifically and the Andean highlands generally, from Inca times to the present, examining the shifting relationship between peasants, indigenous peoples, and the state. Analyzes historically why modern forms of peasant and indigenous political power differs radically between Peru, Bolivia, and Ecuador.
parsedRequirementCourses.sourceHeading: Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces
parsedRequirementCourses.sourceCategory: Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces

parsedRequirementCourses.courseCode: HSTLAC 482 The History Of Brazil: Colonial Period To The Present SSc 5
parsedRequirementCourses.title: Colonial foundations; the first and second empires; the old and new republics; current problems; prospects for the future.
parsedRequirementCourses.sourceHeading: Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces
parsedRequirementCourses.sourceCategory: Provides a historical understanding for major global issues today. Focuses on interdisciplinary social science theories and methods to develop core analytical and writing skills and engage complex questions of causation and effects of global events and forces

parsedRequirementCourses.courseCode: GEOG 403 Transnational Latinx Migrations DIV, SSc 5
parsedRequirementCourses.title: Explores the role of gender, racial formation, and language in transnational Latin American migrations. Outlines key concepts related to power relations in nation-states, geographies of security, border enforcement, and the production of Latinidades, or multiple Latinx identities.
